what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-source channel, pointing to the image resource image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-04 and noting when it was added.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
The target uses a custom image:// URI scheme.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Follows the att-<hash>.md naming pattern for attachments.
References an external asset via a URI-style target rather than embedding it.
concerns: The image:// URI resolution rules are not defined in this file.
There is no alt text, caption, or description for the image.
No parent message or uploader is recorded.
The pitch-deck/p-04 path is opaque without additional context.
notes: A minimal metadata stub that registers a pitch-deck image attachment under the design-source channel.
questions: Which channel message or thread does this attachment belong to?
What does the image at pitch-deck/p-04 depict?
How is the image:// URI resolved to an actual file or blob?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an attachment record and it records an image attachment reference with a target URI and timestamp.
intention_signals: To catalog an image asset shared in the design-source channel so it can be looked up by a stable attachment id and target URI.
novelty: Uses a custom image:// URI scheme to locate channel-specific image assets instead of a conventional filesystem path.
contribution: A machine-readable attachment record that links a design-source channel entry to a specific image asset, providing a stable id, type, target, and addition timestamp.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record for the design-source channel, of type images, pointing to image://design-source/pitch-deck/p-04 and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to register an image attachment in the design-source channel memory so the pitch-deck image p-04 can be referenced by a stable id.
